At a Glance

Kim, here's your complete Alaska cruise package at a glance. We've put together four cruise options — two 7-night and one grand 17-night — all departing from Seattle in early June. You'll fly from Shreveport using your vouchers, and we've included hotel nights in Seattle on each end as needed. Scroll through each section to compare and tap what works for you.

Next: Flights from Shreveport
Getting There

Flights — Shreveport to Seattle

No nonstop flights exist from Shreveport (SHV) to Seattle (SEA) — all routes connect through Dallas/Fort Worth or Houston. American Airlines and United are the primary carriers on this route. Total travel time is typically 6–9 hours depending on the layover. We'll apply your vouchers to cover these flights for all 3 guests. I'd recommend flying in the day before the cruise departs and targeting a late-return flight so you can stay onboard until disembarkation is complete. Tap the options below that appeal to you, then I'll confirm availability.
⚡ Voucher Note: Your flight vouchers are expected to be available for redemption around the time of booking. I'll confirm exact voucher terms and apply them to all 3 passengers (Kim + 2 parents) — inbound and outbound. Call or email me and I'll handle the redemption process for you.
OUTBOUND — SHREVEPORT → SEATTLE (Day Before Cruise)
AA · Depart SHV ~6:15 AM
Connect Dallas/Fort Worth (DFW) · Arrive SEA ~11:30 AM · ~7 hrs total
→
Arrives Seattle ~11:30 AM
Full afternoon to settle in Seattle before cruise day
AA · Depart SHV ~10:15 AM
Connect Dallas/Fort Worth (DFW) · Arrive SEA ~3:30 PM · ~7.5 hrs total
→
Arrives Seattle ~3:30 PM
Afternoon arrival — Pike Place Market evening
United · Depart SHV ~7:15 AM
Connect Houston (IAH) · Arrive SEA ~1:00 PM · ~8 hrs total
→
Arrives Seattle ~1:00 PM
United MileagePlus — great option if using United miles vouchers
RETURN — SEATTLE → SHREVEPORT (Post-Cruise)
💡 Strategy: Princess disembarkation is typically 7:00–10:00 AM. If we can book an afternoon return flight (~2:00 PM or later), you can skip the extra hotel night on the way home. If only morning flights are available, we'll book a post-cruise hotel night so you're not rushing.
AA · Depart SEA ~2:30 PM
Connect DFW · Arrive SHV ~9:00 PM · No extra hotel needed
→
Home ~9:00 PM
Best option — no rush off the ship
AA · Depart SEA ~9:00 AM
Connect DFW · Arrive SHV ~3:30 PM · Requires early off ship
→
Home ~3:30 PM
Earlier return — but a rushed disembarkation morning
Next: Cruise Option 1 — 7-Night Star Princess
Cruise Option 1 of 3

7-Night Alaska — Star Princess

Newest Ship · Endicott Arm & Dawes Glacier
Star Princess
7 Nights · Roundtrip Seattle · Departs May 31 or June 7, 2026
Starting From · 7 Nights · 3 Guests
☝ TAP TO SELECT THIS CRUISE ✓ SELECTED
from$4,500
~$1,500/person · 3 guests · mini-suite
The Star Princess is the brand-new Sphere class ship — launched 2025, featuring The Dome and The Sphere, a stunning glass-enclosed Piazza, and redesigned cabins with more storage and glass shower doors. This is the most modern ship in Princess's Alaska fleet. The May 31 sailing is the earliest June-adjacent option; June 7 gives you a full week's buffer. The itinerary hits Endicott Arm & Dawes Glacier — dramatic calving ice you watch from your balcony.

Agent's Note: The Star Princess is the ship to be on for a first Alaska cruise — the newest tech, best cabin design, and The Dome glass lounge is extraordinary for glacier viewing. Book the May 31 sailing for more availability on excursions; book June 7 if you want the full 7 days clearly in June. Mini-suites on this ship have the new glass shower doors and redesigned layouts — confirm queen vs twin configuration when booking.
Next: Cruise Option 2 — 7-Night Royal Princess (Glacier Bay)
Cruise Option 2 of 3

7-Night Alaska — Royal Princess

Glacier Bay National Park Access
Royal Princess
7 Nights · Roundtrip Seattle · Weekly June Departures 2026
Starting From · 7 Nights · 3 Guests
☝ TAP TO SELECT THIS CRUISE ✓ SELECTED
from$4,800
~$1,600/person · 3 guests · mini-suite
The Royal Princess sails into Glacier Bay National Park — a UNESCO World Heritage Site and the crown jewel of Alaska cruising. Princess is one of the few lines with guaranteed access. Park rangers board the ship and narrate as you glide past Margerie Glacier. Every cabin on Royal Princess faces the sea with a balcony — there are literally no ocean-view cabins without a balcony on this ship. This is the more classic, intimate Alaska experience.

Royal Princess

The day you'll never forget. Slow cruise into Glacier Bay · Park rangers board the ship · Narration of Margerie and Grand Pacific Glaciers · Watch for humpback whales, sea otters, and bald eagles · Balcony is prime real estate today

Arrive Seattle · Disembark · Airport transfer or hotel check-in
Agent's Note: If Glacier Bay is on the bucket list, this is the cruise. Princess has more Glacier Bay entries than any other cruise line, and the ranger narration experience is genuinely special. Book excursions early — whale watching in Juneau and the White Pass Railway in Skagway sell out fast. The $400 free play credit applies onboard; plan for the casino or specialty dining.
Next: Cruise Option 3 — 7-Night Discovery Princess (Best Value)
Cruise Option 3 of 4 — Best Value

7-Night Alaska — Discovery Princess

Best Price · Endicott Arm · More Ashore Late Night Juneau
Discovery Princess
7 Nights · Roundtrip Seattle · Weekly June Departures 2026
Starting From · 7 Nights · 3 Guests — Lowest Price Option
☝ TAP TO SELECT THIS CRUISE ✓ SELECTED
from$3,600
~$1,200/person · 3 guests · mini-suite · lowest fare
The Discovery Princess is the best value option in this lineup — same 7-night Inside Passage route as the Star Princess but on an established Royal Class ship at a lower fare. It sails weekly from Seattle through the summer season, hitting the exact same ports: Ketchikan, Endicott Arm & Dawes Glacier, Juneau, Skagway, and Victoria. Juneau features a "More Ashore" late-night stay until 9 PM — more time to explore without rushing back. If budget is the priority but you still want a balcony and the full Alaska experience, this is the one.

Agent's Note: The Discovery Princess is a 2022 Royal Class ship — it's not old. Same great Princess service, same balcony mini-suite layout, same ports, just a lower price point than the brand-new Star Princess. The "More Ashore" late-night Juneau stay is actually exclusive to Discovery on this route — so you get extra time in Alaska's capital that the Star Princess doesn't offer. For a 3-person party watching the budget, this is the smart play. I'd still book excursions well in advance.
Next: Cruise Option 4 — Grand 17-Night Ultimate Alaska
Cruise Option 4 of 4

17-Night Ultimate Alaska — Grand Princess

3 Glaciers · 10 Ports · The Grand Adventure
Grand Princess
17 Nights · Roundtrip Seattle · Departs ~May 6, 2026
Starting From · 17 Nights · 3 Guests
☝ TAP TO SELECT THIS CRUISE ✓ SELECTED
from$10,500
~$3,500/person · 3 guests · mini-suite
⚠ Timing Note: The confirmed 17-night Grand Princess sailing departs May 6, 2026 — slightly before "early June" but the only 17-night roundtrip Seattle option in the season. Worth considering if 10+ days is the priority. Please confirm with Princess at (861) 601-9019 if additional 17-night departures are available for June.
This is the ultimate Alaska experience — 17 nights, 10 ports of call, and three days of scenic glacier viewing including Hubbard Glacier, College Fjord, and Glacier Bay National Park. For guests who want to do Alaska once and do it completely, this itinerary is unmatched. You'll cover more of Alaska in 17 days than most people see in a lifetime. The extra hotel nights strategy becomes especially important here — we'll plan Seattle arrivals and departures carefully.

All Day
Kenai Fjords National Park viewing · Sea kayaking · Wildlife boat tours · Access to interior Alaska activities

All Day
Prince William Sound · College Fjord with Harvard, Yale, and Bryn Mawr Glaciers · The most ice-dense single viewing day of the voyage

All Day
Relax, reflect, and enjoy the ship as you sail south through the Inside Passage back toward Seattle

Agent's Note: This is a bucket-list trip — 3 glaciers, 10 ports, and Alaska in full. For parents who may not travel this far again, the 17-night option is the one to remember forever. Note the May 6 departure date — I'd recommend calling Princess directly at (861) 601-9019 to ask if any June 17-night sailings are being added for 2026, as they occasionally release additional dates.
Next: Seattle Hotel Options
Seattle Stays

Seattle Hotel Options

You'll want a hotel the night you arrive in Seattle (day before embarkation) and possibly the night you return (post-cruise, if we can't get a late enough flight). I've selected two great options — one right in downtown near the waterfront, and one a short shuttle ride from Pier 91. Tap the option you prefer and I'll get rates confirmed.
Closest to Pier 91 · Cruise Shuttle Available
Mayflower Park Hotel
405 Olive Way, Downtown Seattle · 3 miles from Pier 91
Starting From · 1–2 Nights
☝ TAP TO SELECT THIS HOTEL ✓ SELECTED
from$350
~$175/night · Cruise season rates
Location
Downtown Seattle
Pier 91 Shuttle
✓ Available — $15/person
Walk to
Pike Place Market, Space Needle
Style
Historic boutique charm
Pier 91 Shuttle Service Downtown Location Near Pike Place Market Oliver's Lounge Onsite
A beloved Seattle landmark with old-world charm, located steps from Pike Place Market and the Westlake Center. Offers a dedicated Pier 91 shuttle service — very convenient for cruise travelers. Comfortable rooms, good restaurant and bar onsite.
Agent's Note: This is my top pick for cruise travelers from out of state. The scheduled shuttle to Pier 91 is $15/person and takes the guesswork out of getting to the ship. Book early — cruise season June rates fill quickly. Request rooms with 2 queen beds or a king + rollaway for 3 guests.
Waterfront Location · Best Views
Hotel Vintage Seattle (Kimpton)
1100 5th Ave, Downtown Seattle · 15 min to Pier 91
Starting From · 1–2 Nights
☝ TAP TO SELECT THIS HOTEL ✓ SELECTED
from$420
~$210/night · Boutique luxury pricing
Brand
Kimpton (IHG)
Style
Wine-themed boutique luxury
To Pier 91
15 min by car/rideshare
Perks
Evening wine hour, pet-friendly
Kimpton Wine Hour (Free) Near Pike Place & Waterfront Boutique Design Concierge Can Arrange Transfer
Seattle's beloved wine-themed boutique hotel with a nightly complimentary wine hour in the lobby. Walking distance to Seattle's waterfront, Pike Place Market, and Pioneer Square. A 15-minute rideshare gets you to Pier 91 on embarkation morning.
Agent's Note: Perfect choice if Kim's parents enjoy a more refined hotel experience. The free nightly wine hour is a nice welcome-to-Seattle touch. IHG loyalty points if applicable. No in-house shuttle to the pier — budget ~$30–40 for an Uber/Lyft to Pier 91.
Next: Shore Excursions by Port
Adventures Ashore

Shore Excursions by Port

Here are the best excursion options at each port — from low-key to full adventure. Prices are per person unless noted. Tap any that interest you and I'll check availability and current Princess pricing. The top excursions (whale watching in Juneau, White Pass Railway in Skagway) sell out months in advance, so we'll want to book these as soon as your cruise is confirmed.
KETCHIKAN — Salmon Capital · Totem Heritage
Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show
Chopping, climbing, axe-throwing competition · Covered outdoor arena · Great for all ages · ~$40–$50/pp
Misty Fjords Flightseeing by Floatplane
Spectacular glacier-carved fjords from above · Floatplane departs from Creek Street dock · ~$250–$350/pp
Totem Heritage Center & Creek Street Walk
Self-guided or narrated tour · World's largest collection of standing totem poles · Historic red-light boardwalk · ~$20–$40/pp
Salmon Fishing Charter
Half-day guided charter · All gear included · Catch silver or king salmon · ~$200–$280/pp
JUNEAU — Glaciers · Whales · Capital City
Whale Watching Catamaran — BOOK EARLY
2.5-hour tour · Point Adolphus whale watching · Humpbacks, orcas, sea lions · Naturalist narration · ~$120–$160/pp · Sells out fast
Mendenhall Glacier & Nugget Falls
Bus to visitor center · Walk to the glacier face · Nugget Falls hike included · ~$30–$60/pp (or self-guided via Uber)
Helicopter & Glacier Walk on Mendenhall
Fly to the glacier then walk on the ice with crampons · Unforgettable experience · ~$350–$500/pp · Limited spots
Mount Roberts Tramway
Gondola to 1,800 ft · Panoramic views of Juneau and the Inside Passage · Nature center at top · ~$35–$45/pp
Gold Panning & History Tour
Pan for real Alaskan gold · Learn Juneau gold rush history · Comfortable for all ages and fitness levels · ~$45–$75/pp
SKAGWAY — Gold Rush · Railway · Wilderness
White Pass & Yukon Route Railway — BOOK EARLY
Vintage narrow-gauge train to the Canadian border · Stunning mountain views and trestles · 3 hours roundtrip · ~$130–$175/pp · One of Alaska's most iconic experiences
Chilkoot Trail Hike (Easy–Moderate)
Walk in the footsteps of gold rush stampeders · Guided portion to Finnegan's Point · Scenic mountain and valley views · ~$60–$100/pp
ATV Wilderness Adventure
Off-road through old coal mining trails · Stunning scenery on Dry Creek River Bed · Control your own ATV · ~$150–$200/pp
Klondike Gold Rush National Historical Park
Ranger-led walking tour of Skagway's gold rush-era boardwalks · Free / nominal fee · Perfect for history lovers
ICY STRAIT POINT — (17-Night Only) · True Wilderness
ZipRider — World's Longest Zip Line
5,330 ft zip line from mountain to the beach · Top speed 60+ mph · Breathtaking views · ~$109–$135/pp
Brown Bear Quest & Wildlife Viewing
Small group boat tour to prime bear habitat · Naturalist guide · Icy Strait is one of Alaska's best bear viewing spots · ~$130–$175/pp
SITKA — (17-Night Only) · Russian Alaska Heritage
Sea Otter & Wildlife Quest Catamaran
Cruise Sitka Sound · Sea otters, bald eagles, Steller sea lions · Naturalist guide · ~$100–$140/pp
Sitka National Historical Park & Raptor Center
Russian Bishop's House · 18 totem poles along forest trail · Alaska Raptor Center with live eagles · ~$30–$55/pp
VICTORIA, B.C. — Evening Port Call
Butchart Gardens Evening Visit
World-famous gardens illuminated at night · 55 acres of stunning floral displays · ~$40–$55 CAD/pp (~$30–$42 USD)
Afternoon Tea at The Empress Hotel
Iconic Victoria tradition · Formal tea service at the Fairmont Empress · ~$80–$120 CAD/pp (~$60–$90 USD) · Reserve ahead
Inner Harbour Self-Guided Walk
Whale watching from the harbour dock · Street performers · Free or nominal cost · Easy stroll for all fitness levels
